heading: McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program
label: 1736o–1 McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002, and not as part of the Food for Peace Act which comprises this chapter.

Implementation of Section 3107 of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002, Relating to Food for Education and Child NutritionMemorandum of President of the United States, , 68 F.R. 12569, provided:Mar. 11, 2003Memorandum for the Secretary of AgricultureEffective upon the publication of this memorandum in the Federal Register, there is established the program relating to food for education and child nutrition authorized by subsection 3107(b) of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 () ( [1736–1]). Pursuant to subsection 3107(d) of the Act, the Department of Agriculture is designated to take actions specified in that subsection. The authorities and duties of the President under section 3107 (except the authority to designate under 3107(d)) are delegated to the Secretary of Agriculture.Public Law 107–1717 U.S.C. 17360–1oIn the implementation of a program for which section 3107 provides, the Secretary of Agriculture shall consult as appropriate with the Food Policy Assistance Council established by section 3 of Executive Order 12752 of , as amended [ note], and such heads of Federal departments and agencies as the Secretary determines appropriate.February 25, 19917 U.S.C. 1691You are authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George W. Bush.

Implementation of Section 3107 of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002, Relating to Food for Education and Child NutritionMemorandum of President of the United States, , 68 F.R. 12569, provided:Mar. 11, 2003Memorandum for the Secretary of AgricultureEffective upon the publication of this memorandum in the Federal Register, there is established the program relating to food for education and child nutrition authorized by subsection 3107(b) of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 () ( [1736–1]). Pursuant to subsection 3107(d) of the Act, the Department of Agriculture is designated to take actions specified in that subsection. The authorities and duties of the President under section 3107 (except the authority to designate under 3107(d)) are delegated to the Secretary of Agriculture.Public Law 107–1717 U.S.C. 17360–1oIn the implementation of a program for which section 3107 provides, the Secretary of Agriculture shall consult as appropriate with the Food Policy Assistance Council established by section 3 of Executive Order 12752 of , as amended [ note], and such heads of Federal departments and agencies as the Secretary determines appropriate.February 25, 19917 U.S.C. 1691You are authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George W. Bush.

children.text: is produced in the United States; or

children.label: i
children.text: a developing country that is a recipient country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a developing country in the same region as a recipient country; and

children.label: B
children.text: at a minimum, meets each nutritional, quality, and labeling standard of the recipient country, as determined by the Secretary.

preschool and school food for education programs in foreign countries to improve food security, reduce the incidence of hunger, and improve literacy and primary education, particularly with respect to girls; and
maternal, infant, and child nutrition programs for pregnant women, nursing mothers, infants, and children who are 5 years of age or younger.

children.heading: Eligible commodities and cost items
children.label: c Eligible commodities and cost items

children.text: any agricultural commodity is eligible to be provided under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: the cost of acquiring agricultural commodities;

children.label: ii
children.text: the costs associated with packaging, enrichment, preservation, and fortification of agricultural commodities;

children.label: iii
children.text: the processing, transportation, handling, and other incidental costs up to the time of the delivery of agricultural commodities free on board vessels in United States ports;

children.label: iv
children.text: the vessel freight charges from United States ports or designated Canadian transshipment ports, as determined by the Secretary, to designated ports of entry abroad;

children.label: I
children.text: of landlocked countries;

children.label: II
children.text: of ports that cannot be used effectively because of natural or other disturbances;

children.label: III
children.text: of the unavailability of carriers to a specific country; or

children.label: IV
children.text: of substantial savings in costs or time that may be effected by the utilization of points of entry other than ports;

children.label: vi
children.text: the costs associated with transporting the commodities described in subsection (a)(2) from a developing country described in subparagraph (A)(ii) of that subsection to any designated point of entry within the recipient country; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the charges for general average contributions arising out of the ocean transport of agricultural commodities transferred pursuant thereto;

children.label: i
children.text: payment of the costs is appropriate; and

children.label: I
children.text: meets the poverty criteria established by the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development for Civil Works Preference; and

children.label: II
children.text: has a national government that is committed to or is working toward, through a national action plan, the goals of the World Declaration on Education for All convened in 1990 in Jomtien, Thailand, and the followup Dakar Framework for Action of the World Education Forum, convened in 2000;

children.label: C
children.text: the costs of activities conducted in the recipient countries by a nonprofit voluntary organization, cooperative, or intergovernmental agency or organization that would enhance the effectiveness of the activities implemented by such entities under this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: the costs of meeting the allowable administrative expenses of private voluntary organizations, cooperatives, or intergovernmental organizations that are implementing activities under this section.
implement the program established under this section;
ensure that the program established under this section is consistent with the foreign policy and development assistance objectives of the United States; and

consider, in determining whether a country should receive assistance under this section, whether the government of the country is taking concrete steps to improve the preschool and school systems in the country.

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: e Eligible entities
children.text: Assistance may be provided under this section to private voluntary organizations, cooperatives, intergovernmental organizations, governments of developing countries and their agencies, and other organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: provide for the submission of proposals by eligible entities, each of which may include 1 or more recipient countries, for commodities and other assistance under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: provide for eligible commodities and assistance on a multiyear basis;

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that eligible entities demonstrate the organizational capacity and the ability to develop, implement, monitor, report on, and provide accountability for activities conducted under this section;

children.label: D
children.text: provide for the expedited development, review, and approval of proposals submitted in accordance with this section;

children.label: i
children.text: is provided under this section in a timely manner; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is available when needed throughout the applicable school year;

children.label: F
children.text: ensure monitoring and reporting by eligible entities on the use of commodities and other assistance provided under this section; and

children.label: G
children.text: allow for the sale or barter of commodities by eligible entities to acquire funds to implement activities that improve the food security of women and children or otherwise enhance the effectiveness of programs and activities authorized under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: identify and assess the needs of beneficiaries, especially malnourished or undernourished mothers and their children who are 5 years of age or younger, and school-age children who are malnourished, undernourished, or do not regularly attend school;

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of preschool and school-age children, target low-income areas where children’s enrollment and attendance in school is low or girls’ enrollment and participation in preschool or school is low, and incorporate developmental objectives for improving literacy and primary education, particularly with respect to girls; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of programs to benefit mothers and children who are 5 years of age or younger, coordinate supplementary feeding and nutrition programs with existing or newly-established maternal, infant, and children programs that provide health-needs interventions, including maternal, prenatal, and postnatal and newborn care;

children.label: C
children.text: involve indigenous institutions as well as local communities and governments in the development and implementation of the programs and activities to foster local capacity building and leadership; and

children.label: D
children.text: carry out multiyear programs that foster local self-sufficiency and ensure the longevity of programs in the recipient country.

children.heading: Use of Food and Nutrition Service
children.label: g Use of Food and Nutrition Service
children.text: The Food and Nutrition Service of the Department of Agriculture may provide technical advice on the establishment of programs under subsection (b)(1) and on implementation of the programs in the field in recipient countries.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is urged to engage existing international food aid coordinating mechanisms to ensure multilateral commitments to, and participation in, programs similar to programs supported under this section.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 2 Reports
children.text: The Secretary shall annually submit to the Committee on International Relations and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report on the commitments and activities of governments, including the United States government, in the global effort to reduce child hunger and increase school attendance.

children.heading: Private sector involvement
children.label: i Private sector involvement
children.text: The Secretary is urged to encourage the support and active involvement of the private sector, foundations, and other individuals and organizations in programs assisted under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: sustain the benefits to the education, enrollment, and attendance of children in schools in the targeted communities when the provision of commodities and assistance to a recipient country under a program under this section terminates; and

children.label: B
children.text: estimate the period of time required until the recipient country or eligible organization is able to provide sufficient assistance without additional assistance under this section; or
to provide other long-term benefits to targeted populations of the recipient country.

children.heading: Requirement to safeguard local production and usual marketing
children.label: k Requirement to safeguard local production and usual marketing
children.text: The requirement of applies with respect to the availability of commodities under this section.section 1733(a) of this title

children.heading: Use of Commodity Credit Corporation funds
children.label: 1 Use of Commodity Credit Corporation funds
children.text: Of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall use to carry out this section $84,000,000 for fiscal year 2009, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2023.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 3 Administrative expenses
children.text: Funds made available to carry out this section may be used to pay the administrative expenses of the Department of Agriculture or any other Federal agency assisting in the implementation of this section.

children.heading: Purchase of commodities
children.label: 4 Purchase of commodities
children.text: Of the funds made available to carry out this section, not more than 10 percent shall be used to purchase agricultural commodities described in subsection (a)(2).
